347	%  By induction hypothesis we see that the
348	%  Auslander-Buchsbaum formula holds for \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\). Since the
349	%  depths of both \(R/xR\) and \(M/xM\) have decreased by one and the projective
350	%  dimension has not changed we conclude."
351	\begin{proof}
352	  
353	  We induct on \(\text{depth}(M)\).
354	
355	  \emph{Base case \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\).} Let \(e := \text{pd}_R(M)\) and pick a minimal
356	  finite free resolution
357	  \[
358	    0 \longrightarrow R^{n_e} \longrightarrow R^{n_{e-1}} \longrightarrow
359	    \cdots \longrightarrow R^{n_0} \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
360	  \]
361	  with all transition maps having matrix coefficients in \(\mathfrak{m}\) (this is the
362	  ``minimal-resolution-after-trimming-trivial-summands'' normalisation, cf.\ Stacks
363	  \texttt{lemma-add-trivial-complex}). On the one hand, the ``what is exact'' criterion
364	  (Stacks tag 00MF, \texttt{proposition-what-exact}, expressing exactness of a sequence of
365	  finite free modules in terms of depths of ideals of \(r\)-minors) gives
366	  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq e\). On the other hand, splitting the resolution into short exact
367	  sequences
368	  \[
369	    0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to K_{e-2} \to 0, \quad
370	    0 \to K_{e-2} \to R^{n_{e-2}} \to K_{e-3} \to 0, \quad \dots, \quad
371	    0 \to K_0 \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0,
372	  \]
373	  and applying Lemma~REF iteratively,
374	  yields the chain of inequalities
375	  \(\text{depth}(K_{e-2}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 1\),
376	  \(\text{depth}(K_{e-3}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 2\), \dots,
377	  \(\text{depth}(K_0) \geq \text{depth}(R) - (e-1)\), and finally
378	  \(\text{depth}(M) \geq \text{depth}(R) - e\). Since \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\) we deduce
379	  \(\text{depth}(R) \leq e\). Combined with the previous inequality, $\text{depth}(R) = e =
380	  \text{pd}_R(M) + 0 = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)$.
381	
382	  \emph{Inductive step \(\text{depth}(M) > 0\).} We claim there exists \(x \in \mathfrak{m}\)
383	  which is a non-zero-divisor on both \(R\) and \(M\). Indeed: either \(\text{pd}_R(M) > 0\), in
384	  which case \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) by the ``what is exact'' criterion applied to the start
385	  of a minimal resolution, or \(\text{pd}_R(M) = 0\) in which case \(M\) is finite free and
386	  \(\text{depth}(R) = \text{depth}(M) > 0\). Either way both \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) and
387	  \(\text{depth}(M) > 0\), hence \(\text{depth}(R \oplus M) > 0\) by
388	  Lemma~REF, so a common non-zero-divisor $x \in
389	  \mathfrak{m}\( exists. Take a minimal finite free resolution of \)M$ as above; the snake
390	  lemma applied to multiplication by \(x\) gives a minimal finite free resolution
391	  \[
392	    0 \to (R/xR)^{n_e} \to (R/xR)^{n_{e-1}} \to \cdots \to (R/xR)^{n_0} \to M/xM \to 0
393	  \]
394	  of \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\), of the same length \(e\). So
395	  \(\text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) = \text{pd}_R(M)\). By Stacks
396	  \texttt{lemma-depth-drops-by-one}, both \(\text{depth}(R/xR) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\) and
397	  \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1\), and one verifies
398	  \(\text{depth}_R(M/xM) = \text{depth}_{R/xR}(M/xM)\) (regular sequences in \(\mathfrak{m}\)
399	  correspond to regular sequences in \(\mathfrak{m}/(x)\)). Apply the inductive hypothesis to
400	  \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\):
401	  \[
402	    \text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) + \text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(R/xR).
403	  \]
404	  Substituting yields
405	  \(\text{pd}_R(M) + (\text{depth}(M) - 1) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\), i.e.\
406	  \(\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M) = \text{depth}(R)\), as required.
407	\end{proof}

409	\section{Corollary: regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay}
410	\label{sec:ab_regular_cm}
411	
412	\begin{definition}
413	
414	\leanok
415	  [Cohen--Macaulay local ring]
416	  \label{def:cohen_macaulay_local}
417	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay}
418	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM)
419	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25288-L25299).
420	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
421	  % "A Noetherian local ring \(R\) is called {\it Cohen-Macaulay}
422	  %  if it is Cohen-Macaulay as a module over itself."
423	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM).}
424	  A Noetherian local ring \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is called \emph{Cohen--Macaulay} if
425	  \(\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)\) (equivalently, if \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay as a module
426	  over itself, where Cohen--Macaulay for a finite \(R\)-module \(M\) means
427	  \(\dim(\text{Supp}(M)) = \text{depth}(M)\), cf.\ Stacks tag 00N3).
428	\end{definition}
429

433	% L1096 — line drifted from iter-184's L944 reference) that now carries a
434	% structural induction scaffold via `regularLocal_inductive_step`, with the

445	% iter-186+ work paths unchanged: (a) formalise Stacks 00NQ in project
446	% (~300 LOC); (b) Koszul-homology bypass via `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`;
447	% (c) Mathlib upstream.
448	\begin{corollary}
449	
450	\leanok
451	  [Regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay]
452	  \label{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
453	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular}
454	  
455	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM)
456	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25714-L25730).
457	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
458	  % "Let \(R\) be a regular local ring and let
459	  %  \(x_1, \ldots, x_d\) be a minimal set of generators
460	  %  for the maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\). Then
461	  %  \(x_1, \ldots, x_d\) is a regular sequence, and
462	  %  each \(R/(x_1, \ldots, x_c)\) is a regular local ring
463	  %  of dimension \(d - c\). In particular \(R\) is Cohen-Macaulay."
464	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM).}
465	  Every regular Noetherian local ring is Cohen--Macaulay: if \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is
466	  regular local of Krull dimension \(d\), then \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)\).
467	\end{corollary}
468	
469	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
470	% "Note that \(R/x_1R\) is a Noetherian local ring of dimension \(\geq d - 1\)
471	%  by Lemma REF with \(x_2, \ldots, x_d\)
472	%  generating the maximal ideal. Hence it is a regular local ring by definition.
473	%  Since \(R\) is a domain by Lemma REF
474	%  \(x_1\) is a nonzerodivisor."
475	\begin{proof}
476	  
477	  
478	\leanok
479	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be regular of dimension \(d\) and pick a minimal generating set
480	  \(x_1, \dots, x_d\) of \(\mathfrak{m}\). By Stacks tag 00NQ (lemma-regular-domain), \(R\) is
481	  an integral domain, so \(x_1\) is a non-zero-divisor. The quotient \(R/x_1 R\) is a Noetherian
482	  local ring of dimension \(\geq d - 1\) (by Krull's principal ideal theorem,
483	  \texttt{lemma-one-equation}) with maximal ideal generated by \(x_2, \dots, x_d\), hence
484	  has dimension exactly \(d - 1\) and is again regular of dimension \(d - 1\). Inducting on \(d\),
485	  \(x_1, \dots, x_d\) is an \(R\)-regular sequence, so
486	  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq d\). The reverse inequality \(\text{depth}(R) \leq \dim(R) = d\)
487	  always holds for any nonzero finite module over a Noetherian local ring
488	  (Stacks tag 00LK, \texttt{lemma-bound-depth}). Thus \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)\), i.e.\
489	  \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay.
490	
491	  \emph{Remark on the Auslander--Buchsbaum route.} The same conclusion can be obtained from
492	  Theorem~REF as follows: take \(M := R\) itself. Then \(M\) is
493	  finitely generated and projective (free of rank \(1\)), so \(\text{pd}_R(R) = 0\). The
494	  formula gives \(\text{depth}(R) = 0 + \text{depth}(R)\), a tautology. The non-tautological
495	  use of Auslander--Buchsbaum is rather: for a regular local ring of dimension \(d\), every
496	  finitely generated module \(M\) has \(\text{pd}_R(M) \leq d\) (Stacks tag 00O2,
497	  \texttt{proposition-finite-gl-dim-regular}); applying the formula to such an \(M\) then
498	  relates \(\text{depth}(M)\) to \(d - \text{pd}_R(M)\), which is the form most often used in
499	  practice. The direct regular-sequence argument above is the cleanest proof of
500	  \(R\) Cohen--Macaulay itself.
501	\end{proof}

503	\section{Application: codim-1 extension on a regular surface (gating A.4.a)}
504	\label{sec:ab_application_to_a4a}
505	
506	The downstream consumer is A.4.a: extending a rational map
507	\(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) from a regular projective surface \(S\) to an abelian variety \(A\)
508	across a closed point \(x \in S\) that is missing from the domain of definition \(U \subseteq S\).
509	The classical mechanism (cf.\ Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, III.7) is:
510	
511	\begin{enumerate}
512	  \item The local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\) is a regular Noetherian local ring of Krull
513	    dimension \(2\) (because \(S\) is regular and \(\dim S = 2\)).
514	  \item By Corollary~REF it is Cohen--Macaulay, so
515	    \(\text{depth}(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}) = 2\). Equivalently, the maximal ideal contains an
516	    \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\)-regular sequence of length \(2\) (any two transverse generators).
517	  \item Sections of the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_S\) on \(U = S \setminus \{x\}\) extend
518	    uniquely across \(x\) because \(H^0_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) and
519	    \(H^1_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) (these local-cohomology vanishings are equivalent to depth
520	    \(\geq 2\) at \(x\) by Stacks tag 0AVF; see Hartshorne, III.3.5, for the surface case).
521	  \item For a rational map \(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) with codimension-\(\geq 2\) indeterminacy
522	    locus on a regular surface, the same depth-\(\geq 2\) input produces a sheaf-theoretic
523	    extension of \(f\) to all of \(S\): pull back any sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_A\)-affine-locally,
524	    obtain sections over \(U\), and extend across \(x\) by the above.
525	\end{enumerate}
526	
527	So Corollary~REF is the gating input: the geometric A.4.a
528	proof can be written conditional on the algebraic Auslander--Buchsbaum corollary, and the
529	two halves develop independently --- as long as Theorem~REF and
530	Corollary~REF are formalized in
531	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean} with the signatures pinned
532	in this chapter, A.4.a can consume them as Mathlib-style imports.
533

799	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
800	
801	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
802	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
803	```
804	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
805	```
806	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
807	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
808	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
809	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
810	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
811	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
812	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
813	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
814	
815	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/

820	```
821	  pd_R(M) + depth_R(M) = depth(R).
822	```
823

825	upper bound `n : ℕ` on the projective dimension (so the formula compares

845	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings
846	
847	A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth equals
848	its Krull dimension (Stacks tag 00N4). Mathlib `b80f227` has neither the
849	predicate nor the class — this file is the upstream gap-fill.
850

872	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
873	
874	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
875	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
876	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
877	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
878	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
879	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
880	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
881	
882	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
883	
884	namespace CohenMacaulay
885	
886	/-! ### Helper 1 (axiom-clean): length-bound on regular sequences
887	
888	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, every `R`-regular sequence has length at most
889	`ringKrullDim R`. This is the **upper bound** half of Stacks 00OD: it is the
890	specialisation of the equality
891	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) + rs.length = ringKrullDim R`
892	(`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) to the observation that
893	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) ≥ 0` whenever the quotient is nontrivial, which it
894	is precisely because `IsRegular` rules out `rs • ⊤ = ⊤`.
895	
896	iter-181 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. -/
897	private lemma length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular
898	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
899	    {rs : List R} (h : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs) :
900	    (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim R := by
901	  have heq := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs h
902	  have hntq : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) := by
903	    rw [Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
904	    intro habs
905	    apply h.top_ne_smul
906	    change (⊤ : Submodule R R) = (Ideal.ofList rs) • ⊤
907	    rw [habs]; simp
908	  have hnn : (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) :=
909	    ringKrullDim_nonneg_of_nontrivial
910	  calc (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞)
911	      = 0 + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by simp
912	    _ ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by gcongr
913	    _ = ringKrullDim R := heq

1008	private lemma regularLocal_inductive_step {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1009	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ℕ}
1010	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
1011	    (IH : ∀ (R' : Type u) [CommRing R'] [IsLocalRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R']
1012	            [IsRegularLocalRing R'],
1013	            (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').spanFinrank = k →
1014	            ∃ rs : List R', rs.length = k ∧
1015	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') ∧
1016	              RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs) :
1017	    ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = k + 1 ∧
1018	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
1019	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs := by
1020	  -- Step 1: extract `x ∈ 𝔪` regular on `R` with `R ⧸ (x)` regular local of
1021	  -- `spanFinrank = k`.  This is the consolidated Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate.
1022	  obtain ⟨x, hxMem, hxReg, hRLR, hdim_quot⟩ :=
1023	    exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ hdim
1024	  -- Step 2: apply IH on `R ⧸ (x)` — this gives a regular sequence of length

1026	  obtain ⟨rs'_q, hlen_q, hmem_q, hreg_q⟩ := IH (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) hdim_quot

1035	    change (rs'_q.map g).map mkq = rs'_q
1036	    rw [List.map_map]
1037	    conv_rhs => rw [← List.map_id rs'_q]

1157	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective
1158	surface): the local ring `O_{S,x}` of a regular projective surface at a

1174	  depth_eq_krullDim := by
1175	    -- Step 1: simplify `Module.depth` via the `else` branch
1176	    --   (since `𝔪 • ⊤ = 𝔪 ≠ ⊤` for a local ring's maximal ideal).
1177	    have h𝔪 : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R R)
1178	        ≠ (⊤ : Submodule R R) := by
1179	      have heq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R R)
1180	          = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by simp
1181	      rw [heq]

1183	    rw [Module.depth, if_neg h𝔪]
1184	    -- Step 2: convert RHS to the spanFinrank using
1185	    -- `IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`.

1187	    -- Goal: ((sSup {n | …} : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞)
1188	    --         = ((spanFinrank 𝔪 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)
1189	    -- Step 3: it suffices to show the sSup equals spanFinrank as ℕ∞,

1192	        (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1193	          ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs }
1194	        : ℕ∞) = ((IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank : ℕ∞) := by

1199	        have hub := length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular hreg
1200	        rw [← IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal] at hub
1201	        exact_mod_cast hub
1202	      · -- Lower bound: spanFinrank is achieved by Helper 2's regular sequence.
1203	        obtain ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩ := exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal R
1204	        apply le_sSup
1205	        refine ⟨rs, ?_, hmem, hreg⟩
1206	        exact_mod_cast hlen
1207	    rw [h1]
1208	    -- Final coercion: `((n : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) = ((n : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)`
1209	    -- is the standard `Nat.cast`-tower commutation.
1210	    rfl

205	/-- The map `I/I² → J/J²` if `I ≤ f⁻¹(J)`. -/
206	def mapCotangent (I₁ : Ideal A) (I₂ : Ideal B) (f : A →ₐ[R] B) (h : I₁ ≤ I₂.comap f) :
207	    I₁.Cotangent →ₗ[R] I₂.Cotangent := by
208	  refine Submodule.mapQ ((I₁ • ⊤ : Submodule A I₁).restrictScalars R)
209	    ((I₂ • ⊤ : Submodule B I₂).restrictScalars R) ?_ ?_
210	  · exact f.toLinearMap.restrict (p := I₁.restrictScalars R) (q := I₂.restrictScalars R) h
211	  · intro x hx
212	    rw [Submodule.restrictScalars_mem] at hx
213	    refine Submodule.smul_induction_on hx ?_ (fun _ _ ↦ add_mem)
214	    rintro a ha ⟨b, hb⟩ -
215	    simp only [SetLike.mk_smul_mk, smul_eq_mul, Submodule.mem_comap, Submodule.restrictScalars_mem]
216	    convert (Submodule.smul_mem_smul (M := I₂) (r := f a)
217	      (n := ⟨f b, h hb⟩) (h ha) (Submodule.mem_top)) using 1
218	    ext
219	    exact map_mul f a b

224	    Ideal.mapCotangent I₁ I₂ f h (Ideal.toCotangent I₁ x) = Ideal.toCotangent I₂ ⟨f x, h x.2⟩ := rfl

233	/-- Lift a linear map `f : I →ₗ[R] M` that vanishes on products to a linear map on the
234	cotangent space `I ⧸ I ^ 2`. -/
235	def lift (f : I →ₗ[R] M) (hf : ∀ (x y : I), f (x * y) = 0) :
236	    I.Cotangent →ₗ[R] M where
237	  __ := QuotientAddGroup.lift _ f.toAddMonoidHom <| fun x hx ↦ by
238	    simp only [Submodule.mem_toAddSubgroup, AddMonoidHom.mem_ker] at hx ⊢
239	    refine Submodule.smul_induction_on hx (fun r hr y _ ↦ hf ⟨r, hr⟩ y) fun x y hx hy ↦ ?_
240	    simp only [map_add, hx, hy, add_zero]
241	  map_smul' r x := by
242	    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := I.toCotangent_surjective x
243	    exact map_smul f _ _
244	
245	@[simp]
246	lemma lift_toCotangent (f : I →ₗ[R] M) (hf : ∀ (x y : I), f (x * y) = 0) (x : I) :
247	    Cotangent.lift f hf (I.toCotangent x) = f x :=
248	  rfl
249	
250	@[simp]
251	lemma lift_comp_toCotangent (f : I →ₗ[R] M) (hf : ∀ (x y : I), f (x * y) = 0) :
252	    Cotangent.lift f hf ∘ₗ I.toCotangent = f :=
253	  rfl
254	
255	lemma lift_surjective_iff (f : I →ₗ[R] M) (hf : ∀ (x y : I), f (x * y) = 0) :
256	    Function.Surjective (Cotangent.lift f hf) ↔ Function.Surjective f := by
257	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
258	  · rw [← Cotangent.lift_comp_toCotangent f hf, LinearMap.coe_comp]
259	    exact Function.Surjective.comp h (toCotangent_surjective I)
260	  · dsimp [Cotangent.lift]
261	    exact QuotientAddGroup.lift_surjective_of_surjective _ _ h _
262	
263	end Lift
264	
265	/-- A linear isomorphism between cotangent spaces induced by an equality of ideals. -/
266	@[expose]
267	def equivOfEq (I J : Ideal R) (hIJ : I = J) :
268	    I.Cotangent ≃ₗ[R] J.Cotangent where
269	  __ := Cotangent.lift (J.toCotangent ∘ₗ LinearEquiv.ofEq I J hIJ) <| fun x y ↦ by
270	    simp [toCotangent_eq_zero, ← hIJ, sq, mul_mem_mul]
271	  invFun := Cotangent.lift (I.toCotangent ∘ₗ LinearEquiv.ofEq J I hIJ.symm) <| fun x y ↦ by
272	    simp [toCotangent_eq_zero, hIJ, sq, mul_mem_mul]
273	  left_inv x := by
274	    subst hIJ
275	    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := I.toCotangent_surjective x
276	    simp
277	  right_inv x := by
278	    subst hIJ
279	    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := I.toCotangent_surjective x
280	    simp
281	
282	@[simp]
283	lemma equivOfEq_toCotangent (I J : Ideal R) (hIJ : I = J) (x : I) :
284	    Cotangent.equivOfEq I J hIJ (I.toCotangent x) = J.toCotangent (LinearEquiv.ofEq I J hIJ x) :=
285	  rfl
286	
287	@[simp]
288	lemma equivOfEq_symm (I J : Ideal R) (hIJ : I = J) :
289	    (Cotangent.equivOfEq I J hIJ).symm = Cotangent.equivOfEq J I hIJ.symm :=
290	  rfl
291	
292	end Ideal.Cotangent

305	  inferInstanceAs <| IsScalarTower R (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) _

309	  Module.Finite.of_restrictScalars_finite R _ _

313	lemma subsingleton_cotangentSpace_iff [IsNoetherianRing R] :
314	    Subsingleton (CotangentSpace R) ↔ IsField R := by
315	  refine (maximalIdeal R).cotangent_subsingleton_iff.trans ?_
316	  rw [IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq,
317	    Ideal.isIdempotentElem_iff_eq_bot_or_top_of_isLocalRing]

320	lemma CotangentSpace.map_eq_top_iff [IsNoetherianRing R] {M : Submodule R (maximalIdeal R)} :
321	    M.map (maximalIdeal R).toCotangent = ⊤ ↔ M = ⊤ := by
322	  refine ⟨fun H ↦ eq_top_iff.mpr ?_, by rintro rfl; simp [Ideal.toCotangent_range]⟩
323	  refine (Submodule.map_le_map_iff_of_injective (Submodule.injective_subtype _) _ _).mp ?_
324	  rw [Submodule.map_top, Submodule.range_subtype]
325	  apply Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot (IsNoetherian.noetherian _)
326	    (IsLocalRing.jacobson_eq_maximalIdeal _ bot_ne_top).ge
327	  rw [smul_eq_mul, ← pow_two, ← Ideal.map_toCotangent_ker, ← Submodule.map_sup,
328	    ← Submodule.comap_map_eq, H, Submodule.comap_top, Submodule.map_top, Submodule.range_subtype]

330	lemma CotangentSpace.span_image_eq_top_iff [IsNoetherianRing R] {s : Set (maximalIdeal R)} :
331	    Submodule.span (ResidueField R) ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent '' s) = ⊤ ↔
332	      Submodule.span R s = ⊤ := by
333	  rw [← map_eq_top_iff, ← (Submodule.restrictScalars_injective R ..).eq_iff,
334	    Submodule.restrictScalars_span]
335	  · simp only [Ideal.toCotangent_apply, Submodule.restrictScalars_top, Submodule.map_span]
336	  · exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective

350	    finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) ≤ 1 ↔ (maximalIdeal R).IsPrincipal := by
351	  rw [Module.finrank_le_one_iff_top_isPrincipal, isPrincipal_iff,
352	    (maximalIdeal R).toCotangent_surjective.exists, isPrincipal_iff]
353	  simp_rw [← Set.image_singleton, eq_comm (a := ⊤), CotangentSpace.span_image_eq_top_iff,
354	    ← (map_injective_of_injective (injective_subtype _)).eq_iff, map_span, Set.image_singleton,
355	    Submodule.map_top, range_subtype, eq_comm (a := maximalIdeal R)]
356	  exact ⟨fun ⟨x, h⟩ ↦ ⟨_, h⟩, fun ⟨x, h⟩ ↦ ⟨⟨x, h ▸ subset_span (Set.mem_singleton x)⟩, h⟩⟩

361	
362	lemma Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq (surj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap A B))
363	    {I : Ideal B} {J : Ideal A} (eq : I.comap (algebraMap A B) = RingHom.ker (algebraMap A B) ⊔ J) :
364	    Function.Surjective (Ideal.mapCotangent J I (Algebra.ofId A B)
365	      (le_of_le_of_eq le_sup_right eq.symm)) := by
366	  intro x
367	  rcases I.toCotangent_surjective x with ⟨x', rfl⟩
368	  rcases Ideal.exists_of_comap_eq_ker_sup _ surj eq x'.2 with ⟨y', mem, hy'⟩
369	  use J.toCotangent ⟨y', mem⟩
370	  simpa using I.toCotangent.congr_arg (SetCoe.ext hy')
371	
372	lemma Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective (surj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap A B))
373	    {I : Ideal B} {J : Ideal A} (eq : I.comap (algebraMap A B) = RingHom.ker (algebraMap A B) ⊔ J) :
374	    (Ideal.mapCotangent J I (Algebra.ofId A B) (le_of_le_of_eq le_sup_right eq.symm)).ker =
375	      (Submodule.comap J.subtype ((RingHom.ker (algebraMap A B)) ⊓ J)).map J.toCotangent := by
376	  have eqmap := Ideal.eq_map_of_comap_eq_ker_sup _ surj eq
377	  refine le_antisymm (fun x hx ↦ ?_) ?_
378	  · rcases J.toCotangent_surjective x with ⟨x', hx'⟩
379	    have : Function.Surjective (Algebra.ofId A B) := surj
380	    simp only [← hx', LinearMap.mem_ker, Ideal.mapCotangent_toCotangent,
381	      Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero, eqmap, Algebra.ofId_apply] at hx
382	    rw [← Ideal.map_pow, ← Ideal.mem_comap, Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective' _ surj] at hx
383	    rcases Submodule.mem_sup.mp hx with ⟨y, hy, z, hz, hyz⟩
384	    have : y + z ∈ J := by simp [hyz]
385	    have zmemJ := (Ideal.add_mem_iff_right J (Ideal.pow_le_self (by omega) hy)).mp this
386	    have xeq : x = J.toCotangent ⟨z, zmemJ⟩ := by simpa [← hx', J.toCotangent_eq, ← hyz] using hy
387	    rw [xeq]
388	    exact Submodule.mem_map_of_mem (Submodule.mem_comap.mpr (Ideal.mem_inf.mpr ⟨hz, zmemJ⟩))
389	  · rw [Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap, ← LinearMap.ker_comp]
390	    intro x hx
391	    simp only [LinearMap.mem_ker, LinearMap.comp_apply, Ideal.mapCotangent_toCotangent]
392	    convert map_zero I.toCotangent
393	    exact (Ideal.mem_inf.mp hx).1

12	/-!
13	# Span rank under operations
14	
15	In this file we show how operations on submodules interact with `Submodule.spanRank`.
16	
17	# Main Results
18	
19	* `Submodule.spanRank_baseChange_le`: Base change doesn't increase the span rank.
20	
21	* `TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField`: For a finitely generated module over
22	  a local ring, the dimension of the base change to the residue field is equal to its span rank.
23	
24	* `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`: The minimal number of
25	  generators of the unique maximal ideal is equal to the dimension of the cotangent space.
26	
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public section
30	
31	open IsLocalRing TensorProduct Submodule
32	
33	variable {R A : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
34	  {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (N : Submodule R M)
35	
36	lemma Submodule.spanRank_baseChange_le : (N.baseChange A).spanRank ≤ N.spanRank.lift := by
37	  obtain ⟨s, hs₁, hs₂⟩ := N.exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank
38	  grw [← hs₁, ← hs₂, baseChange_span, spanRank_span_le_card]
39	  convert Cardinal.mk_image_le_lift (f := TensorProduct.mk R A M 1) (s := s)
40	  · exact (Cardinal.lift_id' _).symm
41	  · exact Cardinal.lift_umax.symm
42	
43	lemma Submodule.FG.spanFinrank_baseChange_le (fg : N.FG) :
44	    (N.baseChange A).spanFinrank ≤ N.spanFinrank := by
45	  grw [spanFinrank, spanRank_baseChange_le, Cardinal.toNat_lift, spanFinrank]
46	  simp [Cardinal.lift_lt_aleph0, spanRank_finite_iff_fg.mpr fg]
47	
48	lemma TensorProduct.spanRank_top_le : (⊤ : Submodule A (A ⊗[R] N)).spanRank ≤ N.spanRank.lift := by
49	  grw [← Submodule.baseChange_top, ← N.spanRank_top, spanRank_baseChange_le]
50	
51	lemma TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_le_of_fg (fg : N.FG) :
52	    (⊤ : Submodule A (A ⊗[R] N)).spanFinrank ≤ N.spanFinrank := by
53	  grw [← Submodule.baseChange_top, ← N.spanFinrank_top, (N.fg_top.mpr fg).spanFinrank_baseChange_le]
54	
55	variable [IsLocalRing R]
56	local notation "𝓀" => ResidueField R
57	
58	lemma TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField (fg : N.FG) :
59	    (⊤ : Submodule 𝓀 (𝓀 ⊗[R] N)).spanFinrank = N.spanFinrank := by
60	  let : Module.Finite R N := Module.Finite.iff_fg.mpr fg
61	  apply (TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_le_of_fg N fg).antisymm
62	  obtain ⟨s, hs₁, hs₂⟩ := (⊤ : Submodule 𝓀 (𝓀 ⊗[R] N)).exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank
63	  have hs₃ : s.Finite := Cardinal.mk_lt_aleph0_iff.mp (by simpa [hs₁] using Module.Finite.fg_top)
64	  let t := Function.surjInv (mk_surjective R N 𝓀 residue_surjective) '' s
65	  have ht₁ : mk R 𝓀 N 1 '' t = s := by rw [← Set.image_comp, Function.comp_surjInv, s.image_id]
66	  have ht₂ : span R t = ⊤ := by
67	    rwa [← restrictScalars_eq_top_iff R, restrictScalars_span _ _ (by exact residue_surjective),
68	      ← ht₁, ← map_span, map_tensorProduct_mk_eq_top] at hs₂
69	  grw [← N.spanFinrank_top, ← ht₂, spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (hs₃.image _), spanFinrank,
70	    ← hs₁, Set.ncard_image_le hs₃]
71	  rfl

73	namespace IsLocalRing
74	
75	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
76	lemma spanFinrank_eq_finrank_quotient (N : Submodule R M) (fg : N.FG) :
77	    N.spanFinrank =
78	      Module.finrank (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) (N ⧸ (maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) := by
79	  let : Module 𝓀 (N ⧸ maximalIdeal R • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) :=
80	    inferInstanceAs (Module (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) _)
81	  let : IsScalarTower R 𝓀 (N ⧸ maximalIdeal R • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) :=
82	    inferInstanceAs (IsScalarTower R (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) _)
83	  rw [← spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField N fg, ← Module.finrank_eq_spanFinrank_of_free]
84	  let e : 𝓀 ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ[𝓀] N ⧸ (maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R N) :=
85	    (quotTensorEquivQuotSMul N (maximalIdeal R)).extendScalarsOfSurjective residue_surjective
86	  exact e.finrank_eq
87	
88	lemma spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace_of_fg (fg : (maximalIdeal R).FG) :
89	    (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) :=
90	  spanFinrank_eq_finrank_quotient _ fg
91	
92	variable (R) in
93	lemma spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsNoetherianRing R] :
94	    (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) :=
95	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace_of_fg (maximalIdeal R).fg_of_isNoetherianRing
96	
97	end IsLocalRing

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.SpanRankOperations
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.KrullsHeightTheorem
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.Field
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.PID
13	
14	/-!
15	# Regular local rings
16	
17	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, we define `IsRegularLocalRing` as
18	`(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`. This definition is equivalent to
19	the dimension of the cotangent space over the residue field being equal to `ringKrullDim R`,
20	(see `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
21	
22	# Main Definition and Results
23	
24	* `IsRegularLocalRing` : A Noetherian local ring is regular if
25	  `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`,
26	  i.e. its maximal ideal can be generated by `dim R` elements.
27	
28	* `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` : the equivalence of `IsRegularLocalRing` and
29	  `Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R`
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	open IsLocalRing
36	
37	/-- A Noetherian local ring is said to be regular if its maximal ideal
38	can be generated by `dim R` elements. -/
39	@[stacks 00KU]
40	class IsRegularLocalRing (R : Type*) [CommRing R] : Prop extends
41	    IsLocalRing R, IsNoetherianRing R where
42	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R
43	
44	variable (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
45	
46	lemma isRegularLocalRing_iff [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
47	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R :=
48	  ⟨fun ⟨h⟩ ↦ h, fun h ↦ ⟨h⟩⟩
49	
50	namespace IsRegularLocalRing
51	
52	lemma of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
53	    (le : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank ≤ ringKrullDim R) : IsRegularLocalRing R :=
54	  (isRegularLocalRing_iff _).mpr (le_antisymm le (ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal R))
55	
56	variable {R} in
57	lemma of_ringEquiv [IsRegularLocalRing R] {R' : Type*} [CommRing R']
58	    (e : R ≃+* R') : IsRegularLocalRing R' := by
59	  have := e.isLocalRing
60	  have := isNoetherianRing_of_ringEquiv R e
61	  rwa [isRegularLocalRing_iff, ← ringKrullDim_eq_of_ringEquiv e, ← map_ringEquiv_maximalIdeal e,
62	    Ideal.spanFinrank_map_eq_of_ringEquiv, ← isRegularLocalRing_iff]
63	
64	lemma iff_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
65	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R := by
66	  rw [isRegularLocalRing_iff, spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace]
67	
68	instance [IsLocalRing R] [IsDomain R] [IsPrincipalIdealRing R] : IsRegularLocalRing R := by
69	  by_cases isf : IsField R
70	  · let _ := isf.toField
71	    simp [isRegularLocalRing_iff, maximalIdeal_eq_bot]
72	  · apply of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
73	    rcases IsPrincipalIdealRing.principal (maximalIdeal R) with ⟨x, hx⟩
74	    simpa only [(IsPrincipalIdealRing.ringKrullDim_eq_one R) isf,
75	      Nat.cast_le_one, ← Set.ncard_singleton x, hx] using
76	        Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (Set.finite_singleton x)
77	
78	end IsRegularLocalRing

30	
31	namespace IsLocalRing

33	local notation "p" => maximalIdeal R
34	local notation "pS" => Ideal.map (algebraMap R S) p
35	
36	theorem quotient_span_eq_top_iff_span_eq_top (s : Set S) :
37	    span (R ⧸ p) ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (I := pS)) '' s) = ⊤ ↔ span R s = ⊤ := by
38	  have H : (span (R ⧸ p) ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (I := pS)) '' s)).restrictScalars R =
39	      (span R s).map (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R S (S ⧸ pS) : S →ₗ[R] S ⧸ pS) := by
40	    rw [map_span, ← restrictScalars_span R (R ⧸ p) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective,
41	      LinearMap.coe_coe, IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom', Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq]
42	  constructor
43	  · intro hs
44	    rw [← top_le_iff]
45	    apply le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot
46	    · exact Module.finite_def.mp ‹_›
47	    · exact (jacobson_eq_maximalIdeal ⊥ bot_ne_top).ge
48	    · rw [Ideal.smul_top_eq_map]
49	      rintro x -
50	      have : LinearMap.ker (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R S (S ⧸ pS) : S →ₗ[R] S ⧸ pS) =
51	          Submodule.restrictScalars R pS := by
52	        ext; simp [Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem]
53	      rw [← this, ← comap_map_eq, mem_comap, ← H, hs, restrictScalars_top]
54	      exact mem_top
55	  · intro hs
56	    rwa [hs, Submodule.map_top, LinearMap.range_eq_top.mpr,
57	      restrictScalars_eq_top_iff] at H
58	    rw [LinearMap.coe_coe, IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom', Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq]
59	    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
60	
61	attribute [local instance] Ideal.Quotient.field
62	
63	variable [Module.Free R S] {ι : Type*}
64	
65	theorem finrank_quotient_map :
66	    finrank (R ⧸ p) (S ⧸ pS) = finrank R S := by
67	  classical
68	  have : Module.Finite (R ⧸ p) (S ⧸ pS) := Module.Finite.of_restrictScalars_finite R _ _
69	  apply le_antisymm
70	  · let b := Module.Free.chooseBasis R S
71	    conv_rhs => rw [finrank_eq_card_chooseBasisIndex]
72	    apply finrank_le_of_span_eq_top
73	    rw [Set.range_comp]
74	    apply (quotient_span_eq_top_iff_span_eq_top _).mpr b.span_eq
75	  · let b := Module.Free.chooseBasis (R ⧸ p) (S ⧸ pS)
76	    choose b' hb' using fun i ↦ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective (b i)
77	    conv_rhs => rw [finrank_eq_card_chooseBasisIndex]
78	    refine finrank_le_of_span_eq_top (v := b') ?_
79	    apply (quotient_span_eq_top_iff_span_eq_top _).mp
80	    rw [← Set.range_comp, show Ideal.Quotient.mk pS ∘ b' = ⇑b from funext hb']
81	    exact b.span_eq

83	/-- Given a basis of `S`, the induced basis of `S / Ideal.map (algebraMap R S) p`. -/
84	noncomputable
85	def basisQuotient [Fintype ι] (b : Basis ι R S) : Basis ι (R ⧸ p) (S ⧸ pS) :=
86	  basisOfTopLeSpanOfCardEqFinrank (Ideal.Quotient.mk pS ∘ b)
87	    (by
88	      rw [Set.range_comp]
89	      exact ((quotient_span_eq_top_iff_span_eq_top _).mpr b.span_eq).ge)
90	    (by rw [finrank_quotient_map, finrank_eq_card_basis b])
91	
92	lemma basisQuotient_apply [Fintype ι] (b : Basis ι R S) (i) :
93	    (basisQuotient b) i = Ideal.Quotient.mk pS (b i) := by
94	  delta basisQuotient
95	  rw [coe_basisOfTopLeSpanOfCardEqFinrank, Function.comp_apply]
96	
97	lemma basisQuotient_repr {ι} [Fintype ι] (b : Basis ι R S) (x) (i) :
98	    (basisQuotient b).repr (Ideal.Quotient.mk pS x) i =
99	    Ideal.Quotient.mk p (b.repr x i) := by
100	  refine congr_fun (g := Ideal.Quotient.mk p ∘ b.repr x) ?_ i
101	  apply (Finsupp.linearEquivFunOnFinite (R ⧸ p) _ _).symm.injective
102	  apply (basisQuotient b).repr.symm.injective
103	  simp only [Finsupp.linearEquivFunOnFinite_symm_coe, LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply,
104	    Basis.repr_symm_apply]
105	  rw [Finsupp.linearCombination_eq_fintype_linearCombination_apply (R ⧸ p),
106	    Fintype.linearCombination_apply]
107	  simp only [Function.comp_apply, basisQuotient_apply,
108	    Ideal.Quotient.mk_smul_mk_quotient_map_quotient, ← Algebra.smul_def]
109	  rw [← map_sum, Basis.sum_repr b x]
110	
111	lemma exists_maximalIdeal_pow_le_of_isArtinianRing_quotient
112	    (I : Ideal R) [IsArtinianRing (R ⧸ I)] : ∃ n, maximalIdeal R ^ n ≤ I := by
113	  by_cases hI : I = ⊤
114	  · simp [hI]
115	  have : Nontrivial (R ⧸ I) := Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr hI
116	  have := IsLocalRing.of_surjective' (Ideal.Quotient.mk I) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
117	  have := IsLocalHom.of_surjective (Ideal.Quotient.mk I) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
118	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := IsArtinianRing.isNilpotent_jacobson_bot (R := R ⧸ I)
119	  have : (maximalIdeal R).map (Ideal.Quotient.mk I) = maximalIdeal (R ⧸ I) := by
120	    ext x
121	    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective x
122	    simp [sup_eq_left.mpr (le_maximalIdeal hI)]
123	  rw [jacobson_eq_maximalIdeal _ bot_ne_top, ← this, ← Ideal.map_pow, Ideal.zero_eq_bot,
124	    Ideal.map_eq_bot_iff_le_ker, Ideal.mk_ker] at hn
125	  exact ⟨n, hn⟩
126	
127	lemma finite_quotient_iff [IsNoetherianRing R] [Finite (ResidueField R)] {I : Ideal R} :
128	    Finite (R ⧸ I) ↔ ∃ n, (maximalIdeal R) ^ n ≤ I := by
129	  refine ⟨fun _ ↦ exists_maximalIdeal_pow_le_of_isArtinianRing_quotient I, ?_⟩
130	  rintro ⟨n, hn⟩
131	  have : Finite (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) := ‹_›
132	  have := (Ideal.finite_quotient_pow (IsNoetherian.noetherian (maximalIdeal R)) n)
133	  exact Finite.of_surjective _ (Ideal.Quotient.factor_surjective hn)
134	
135	end IsLocalRing

915	/-! ### Helper 2 (substantive typed `sorry`): system-of-parameters as a regular sequence
916	
917	For a regular local ring `R` of Krull dimension `d = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`,
918	a minimal generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of the maximal ideal `𝔪` is an `R`-regular
919	sequence. This is the **lower bound** half of Stacks 00OD.
920	
921	The proof in Stacks 00OD uses:
922	1. A regular local ring is an integral domain (Stacks 00NQ).
923	2. For each `c < d`, the quotient `R / (x_1, …, x_c)` is again a regular local
924	   ring, of Krull dimension `d - c` (Krull's principal ideal theorem +
925	   `lemma-one-equation`).
926	
927	Step (1) — `IsRegularLocalRing R ⟹ IsDomain R` — is **not present in Mathlib at
928	the pinned commit** (`b80f227`); a `lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is
929	a domain" returns nothing relevant. Step (2) requires the regular-quotient
930	inductive structure (Stacks 00NQ + height-one quotient regularity), which is
931	similarly absent. Both are substantive multi-iter content.
932	
933	The signature here is the non-tautological existence statement: a list `rs : List R`
934	with `rs.length = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`, `rs ⊆ maximalIdeal R`, and
935	`IsRegular R rs`. Downstream, `of_regular` consumes this directly to close the
936	`depth ≥ d` lower bound.
937	
938	iter-185 Lane G **STRUCTURAL SCAFFOLD**: the main lemma is structurally
939	complete by strong induction on `n = spanFinrank R`. The inductive step is
940	factored into `regularLocal_inductive_step` (axiom-clean: lift-and-cons
941	assembly via Mathlib's `IsRegular.cons'`) plus the Mathlib-gap helper
942	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` (typed `sorry`,
943	Stacks 00NQ + 00NU consolidated). The substrate work (`IsRegularLocalRing ⟹
944	IsDomain` + Krull's PIT for quotient-by-minimal-generator) is genuinely
945	multi-iter; the iter-185 progress is the structural extraction of the
946	Mathlib gap into a narrow, well-typed helper, leaving the assembly
947	axiom-clean for the gap-fill iteration to finish in `~5 LOC` once the
948	helper closes. -/
949	
950	/-- **Substrate gap consolidated (Stacks 00NQ + 00NU; typed `sorry`).**
951	For a regular local ring `(R, 𝔪)` of Krull dimension `k + 1`, there exists
952	`x ∈ 𝔪` that is `R`-regular (a non-zero-divisor on `R`) such that the quotient
953	`R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` is again a regular local ring of Krull dimension `k`
954	(equivalently: its maximal ideal has `spanFinrank = k`).
955	
956	The internals combine two Stacks gap-fills, both absent from Mathlib at the
957	pinned commit `b80f227`:
958	
959	* **Stacks 00NQ** — `IsRegularLocalRing R ⟹ IsDomain R` (the no-embedded-prime
960	  argument, ~300 LOC). Once `R` is a domain, every nonzero element is a
961	  non-zero-divisor, so any minimal generator `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` gives
962	  `IsSMulRegular R x`.
963	* **Stacks 00NU** + Krull's principal ideal theorem (~200 LOC). For `x` a
964	  minimal generator of `𝔪` that is a non-zero-divisor, the quotient `R ⧸ (x)`
965	  is again regular local of dimension `k`. Mathlib provides
966	  `ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim` as the dim-drop;
967	  the spanFinrank statement follows by removing `x` from a minimal generating
968	  set of `𝔪` (the images of the remaining `k` generators span `𝔪 / (x)`),
969	  combined with `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le`.
970	
971	iter-185 Lane G: this helper concentrates the Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate

/-! ### Helper 2.0 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent dim-drop on `R ⧸ (x)`

**Statement.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the
cotangent space of `R / (x)` has dimension one less than that of `R`:
```
finrank κ' (CotangentSpace (R/(x))) + 1 = finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
```
where `κ = R / 𝔪` and `κ' = (R/(x)) / 𝔪'` are the two residue fields
(canonically isomorphic via the natural quotient `R/𝔪 ≃ (R/(x))/𝔪'`).

**Role.** This is the cotangent-space dim-drop building block for
`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` below: once Stacks 00NQ
(regular local ⟹ domain) is filled in, this dim-drop is what upgrades
`R/(x)` of dimension `k` back to `IsRegularLocalRing` via
`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.

**Proof structure** (Bourbaki-style via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank`):
1. The R-algebra map `π : R → R/(x)` induces a κ-linear surjection of
   cotangent spaces `f : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent` via
   `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq` (which applies because
   `(maximalIdeal R).comap π = ker π ⊔ maximalIdeal R = (x) ⊔ 𝔪 = 𝔪`).
2. The kernel of `f` is the R-submodule generated by the image `x̄` of `x`
   in `𝔪.Cotangent` (via `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` — the
   kernel is `(Submodule.comap 𝔪.subtype ((x) ⊓ 𝔪)).map 𝔪.toCotangent`
   which collapses to `Submodule.span R {x̄}` because `(x) ⊆ 𝔪`).
3. The R-action on 𝔪.Cotangent factors through κ (it's a κ-vector space),
   so this is also `Submodule.span κ {x̄}`.
4. `x ∉ 𝔪²` ⟺ `x̄ ≠ 0` (Helper 1.5), giving
   `finrank κ (Submodule.span κ {x̄}) = 1` via `finrank_span_singleton`.
5. Combining via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` and the
   `LinearMap.quotKerEquivOfSurjective` isomorphism, we get the formula.
   The residue-field switch `κ → κ'` is handled by
   `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`, which
   reduces the κ-finrank to spanFinrank (a ring-theoretic invariant) and
   the spanFinrank-equality is purely numeric.

933

935	cotangent space of `R / (x)` has dimension one less than that of `R`:
936	```
937	finrank κ' (CotangentSpace (R/(x))) + 1 = finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
938	```
939	where `κ = R / 𝔪` and `κ' = (R/(x)) / 𝔪'` are the two residue fields
940	(canonically isomorphic via the natural quotient `R/𝔪 ≃ (R/(x))/𝔪'`).
941	
942	**Role.** This is the cotangent-space dim-drop building block for
943	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` below: once Stacks 00NQ
944	(regular local ⟹ domain) is filled in, this dim-drop is what upgrades

952	   `(maximalIdeal R).comap π = ker π ⊔ maximalIdeal R = (x) ⊔ 𝔪 = 𝔪`).

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean

## Lane G sub-lane G1 — cotangent-space dim drop helper

### Attempt 1 (iter-187)

**Approach**: Add two new private declarations for the cotangent-space
dim-drop substrate (Lane G sub-lane G1 per iter-187 plan-phase Decision 1):

1. `toCotangent_ne_zero_of_not_mem_sq` (axiom-clean, ~8 LOC at L922–L930):
   for `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` of a local ring `R`, the image of `x` in the cotangent
   space `(maximalIdeal R).Cotangent` is nonzero. Closes via
   `Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero`.

2. `finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ` (1 typed sorry at L1010,
   body ~17 LOC at L984–L1010, plus ~50 LOC docstring detailing the proof
   plan): the main cotangent dim-drop equation
   ```
   finrank κ' (CotangentSpace (R/(x))) + 1 = finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
   ```
   for a Noetherian local ring `R` and `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`. The body reduces the
   κ-finrank statement to a spanFinrank statement via
   `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`
   (axiom-clean rewrite), pushing the substantive content into a single
